economic_notes: Four government business entities accounted for by modified equity method: Takla Development Corporation (logging/silviculture), Sasuchan Development Corporation, Takla Lake Limited Partnership, Tse Keh Nay LLP. COVID-19 pandemic noted as market risk.
notes: Fiscal year 2021 (year ended March 31, 2021). Four GBEs accounted for by modified equity method — Takla Lake Limited Partnership contributed $4,443,470 of the $4,042,556 net income from GBEs (after losses from TDC and Sasuchan). Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($6,132,134) + FNHA ($562,139) + CMHC ($166,559) + Province of BC ($1,319,058) + Carrier Sekani Family Services ($672,562) + Carrier Sekani Tribal Council ($11,240,848) + FNESC ($472,237) + Ottawa Trust Funds ($2,274) = $20,567,811; OSR = own source ($6,893,187) + other interest/rent ($200,696) + change in deferred revenue (-$1,120,285) + GBE income ($4,042,556) = $10,016,154. Annual debt service includes long-term debt repayment ($223,375) and capital lease repayment ($31,482). No schedule of remuneration included in the provided text. Note: total expenses on income statement ($15,772,595) differ from segmented total ($16,125,777) — the income statement figure is used. Prior year surplus includes $90,496 recovery from ISC bringing total to $7,530,776.
